acceptance_criteria: Every cmdlet listed in deliverables is exported.
prd_refs: ┬¦9.8 ŌĆö full cmdlet list and behaviour requirements.
┬¦9.5 ŌĆö naming convention: dot-separated camelCase paths.
description: Expose the public config / policy commands per PRD ┬¦9.8. Setters accept dot-paths (e.g. `model.generationModel`), validate the setting exists unless `-AllowNew`, back up before write (cfg-05), validate the result (cfg-04), and restore on failure. Editors open the file in the user's editor (`$env:EDITOR`/`$env:VISUAL`/`code`/platform default), validate after exit, and offer restore.
notes: Dot-path setter must handle nested objects and arrays ŌĆö write tests for both.
Editor invocation must be mockable; do not call `Start-Process` directly inside the cmdlet ŌĆö use a private helper that tests can override.
steps: TDD: `Get-SlashOpsConfig` returns the user config object.
TDD: `Get-SlashOpsConfig -Raw` returns the raw JSON text.
TDD: `Set-SlashOpsConfig model.generationModel qwen3-coder:30b` round-trips the value.
TDD: `Set-SlashOpsConfig` restores from backup if post-write validation fails.
TDD: `Edit-SlashOpsConfig` opens an editor (mock) and validates after.
TDD: `Reset-SlashOpsConfig` requires confirmation, backs up old config, writes defaults, preserves history.
Author each public cmdlet thin-wrapping a private helper. Reuse `Set-ConfigValue.ps1` for dot-path mutation.
`Edit-*` chooses editor by precedence: `$env:EDITOR` ŌåÆ `$env:VISUAL` ŌåÆ `code` if on PATH ŌåÆ `notepad` (Windows) ŌåÆ `nano` (Linux/macOS).
Confirmation prompts for `Reset-*` honour `-Confirm` / `-Force` ShouldProcess semantics.
Tests use `TestDrive:` and `Mock` for editor invocation.
name: [cfg-08] Public config and policy commands
